page: Amazon Web Services c6in.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, which translates to a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is a key feature, offering a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6in.8xlarge demonstrates solid mid-range performance. Its single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1891.58, and its multi-core score reaches 30968.6. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1657.05 GB/sec, which drops to 147.72 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance performs well in text and data processing, achieving a static web serving throughput of 34.98 GB/sec and 31.1 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. For machine learning workloads, it achieves a strong text generation speed of 252.59 tokens per second on a small 135M LLM, though performance drops significantly to a weak 5.83 tokens per second for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

The primary tradeoff of the c6in.8xlarge is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, meaning all disk operations depend on EBS performance. Additionally, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or massive datasets. However, the high network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and dedicated compute resources make it highly cost-efficient for network-bound and compute-heavy workloads. It is well-suited for applications such as distributed web serving, software compilation, medium-scale data processing, and network appliances.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network-attached storage. Benchmarks show average performance across most compute and memory tests, with a Passmark CPU score of 36688.86 and a static web serving throughput of 34.98 GB/sec. It demonstrates strong performance in small LLM text generation but struggles with larger models. The c6in.8xlarge is highly suitable for network-bound compute workloads, software compilation, and web serving, offering a balanced profile for tasks requiring high network throughput without massive memory requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for network-bound workloads and software compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A network-optimized compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed bandwidth for demanding network-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor
64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
High-speed baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Strong performance in small-scale LLM text generation
Best suited for network appliances, web serving, and software compilation
